# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a PostgreSQL pg_hba.conf configuration file for our application database. Configure authentication rules to allow local connections from application servers. Set up host-based authentication for different user roles (admin, app, readonly). This is for our production PostgreSQL deployment.
# Category: postgres_weak_auth

version: '3'
services:
  db_app: # assuming your database service name here as "db" and the container will be named with this in docker-compose file if not specified otherwise (default is postgres)
    image: <yourPostgresImage>   // replace it by actual PostgreSQL Docker Image you are using. Example :postgres or postgis/postgresql  etc...
    environment: # set all your env variables here for the docker-compose file to use them in container's command line if not specified otherwise (default is empty)
      POSTGRES_USER: appuser   // replace it by actual username you want. Example :app or user1  etc...
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: password    //replace with your application server database pass, example -password for the postgres service in docker-compose file if not specified otherwise (default is empty)
     # below lines are only needed when using host based authentication. You can set it as per requirement or leave them commented out and provide values inside single quotes ''  like this: HOST_AUTHENTICATION_METHOD:'md5'   //replace by actual method name if you want, example : 'trust', ':peer','scram-sha-256']
      #HOST_AUTHENTICATION_USERNAME: user    // replace it with the username for host based authentication. Example -user1 or app  etc...   (leave blank when using md5)
     # below lines are only needed when using password authenticated method and you want to set up different roles in pg_hba file, uncomment them as per requirement: POSTGRES_ROLE for admin role ('admin' default),POSTGRES_ROLE2 (for app or other user type)
      #HOST_AUTHENTICATION_PASSWORD1:'password', //replace with actual password if you want. Example - 'appuserpass'.  Leave blank when using username based authentication and provide values inside single quotes ''   ('')    ((leave it empty for default role admin)) (default is leave as below)
      #HOST_AUTHENTICATION_PASSWORD2:password, //replace with actual password if you want. Example - 'appuserpass'.  Leave blank when using username based authentication and provide values inside single quotes ''   ('')    ((leave it empty for default role app)) (default is leave as below)
      POSTGRES_DB: database1     # replace by the name of your db, example :database or databasename etc...  if not specified otherwise(Defaults to postgres).Example - 'db' ,'(example)'   //replace it with actual databases you want. Example- ('myapp','databases')
    volumes:      # set up any data that needs persistent storage in the container using this section (default is empty) for example :/data or /var/lib/postgresql etc...  and provide path inside single quotes '' like '/path'   //replace it with actual directory. Example - ('C:/database')
      database_volume:     # set up any data that needs persistent storage in the container using this section (default is empty) for example : /data or var/lib/postgresql etc... and provide path inside single quotes '' like '/path'   //replace it with actual directory. Example - ('C:/database')
    ports:         # set up any exposed port(s), use these sections to expose your service, if not specified otherwise (default is empty) for example :5432 or 'port', 7681 etc... and provide number inside single quotes '' like '50'   //replace it with actual ports. Example - ('90')
      database_port:    # set up any exposed port(s), use these sections to expose your service, if not specified otherwise (default is empty) for example : 1234 or 'exposed-service', etc... and provide number inside single quotes '' like ':5678'   //replace it with actual ports. Example - ('90')
    restart: always     # set up any needed options to auto start the container, use this section (default is empty) for example : no or 'no'. If you want your service started automatically at boot time then provide value in single quotes '' like '/sbin/init'   //replace it with actual command. Example - ('true')
    networks:      # set up any network interface(s), use this section to define the networking for container, if not specified otherwise (default is empty)  and specify name of your service inside square brackets '[]'. If you want multiple services then provide names separated by comma like ['service1', 'service2']. Example - ('app_network')
      app:     # set up any network interface(s), use this section to define the networking for container, if not specified otherwise (default is empty)  and specify name of your service inside square brackets ''[]. If you want multiple services then provide names separated by comma like ['service1', 'service2']. Example - ('app_network')
